title: Slow-cooker vegetable soup
subtitle: Root vegetable and leek soup
excerpt: Carrot, potato, parsnip, leek, and celery simmered all day in vegetable stock with thyme until the vegetables are completely soft, then roughly blended for a thick, comforting soup. No frying, no attention, no effort. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Root vegetable soup is a British autumn and winter staple with no single origin, drawing on the tradition of pot-au-feu and minestrone (long-simmered vegetable broths) adapted for British ingredients and the slow cooker. The leek-potato-carrot combination is the standard British winter soup base. Parsnip adds sweetness. The technique of rough blending with a stick blender to a coarse texture is a modern domestic convention.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze for up to three months. Reheat and add a splash of water if too thick.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles well. Keeps for four days in the fridge.

recipeTools.notes: For blending the soup. Leave unblended for a chunkier version.

content.text: This is a true set-and-forget soup. Everything goes in raw at the start — no sautéing, no browning. The resulting flavour is clean and vegetable-forward rather than caramelised, which suits this kind of soup. Blend it to whatever texture you prefer: fully smooth for a restaurant finish, roughly crushed for something more rustic, or not at all for a chunky broth.

Place all the vegetables and garlic into the slow cooker. Pour over {{stock-vegetable}} of vegetable stock. Add the thyme sprigs, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt, and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Cover and cook on LOW for 6–7 hours or HIGH for 3–4 hours until every vegetable is completely soft. Remove the thyme stems. Use a stick blender to blend to the texture you prefer — rough or smooth. Taste for seasoning. Serve with bread.

items.cause: Vegetable stock powder varies enormously in quality and salt level.
items.fix: Taste before serving and add salt generously. A squeeze of lemon juice and a teaspoon of olive oil drizzled into the bowl both lift the flavour.

items.cause: Potato makes a very thick base when fully blended.
items.fix: Stir in boiling water or more stock a ladleful at a time until the texture is right.

items.symptom: Leek pieces very stringy in the soup
items.fix: Blend more thoroughly, or cut the leek into very fine rounds before cooking.

content.text: This soup is a template for whatever root vegetables are in the house. Parsnip gives a sweeter, more fragrant result; celeriac gives earthiness; sweet potato makes it thicker and slightly richer. The base ratio is roughly 1 litre of stock to every 800 g of vegetables. Any combination in that ratio works.
